hypothesis: If a single classifier-free score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state molecular dynamics data and used with score-based interpolation during reverse diffusion followed by Score-Aligned Ascent refinement, then the ASTRA workflow will generate high-committor, saddle-point transition-state configurations across toy potentials, peptide conformational changes, protein folding, and chemical reactions without transition-state training examples, because the interpolated conditional scores approximate the reaction coordinate and SAA projects forces to maximize energy along that coordinate while minimizing orthogonal motion.
